etymology.description: 日(日) (rì, "sun") x 3 = multiple suns shining brightly -> bright; sparkle; crystal
etymology.interpretation: The character 晶(jīng, "sparkle; crystal; bright") is formed by three 日(日) (rì, "sun") characters combined to visually represent multiple suns. This suggests an intense brilliance or sparkle, much like how multiple suns would create a radiant or crystal-like shine.

examples.chinese: 她的眼睛像晶石一样明亮(她的眼睛像晶石一樣明亮)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yǎnjīng xiàng jīngshí yīyàng míngliàng.
examples.english: Her eyes are as bright as crystals.

examples.chinese: 窗户上结了许多冰晶(窗戶上結了許多冰晶)。
examples.pinyin: Chuānghù shàng jié le xǔduō bīngjīng.
examples.english: Many ice crystals have formed on the window.

examples.chinese: 这个城市的夜景闪烁着晶光(這個城市的夜景閃爍著晶光)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège chéngshì de yèjǐng shǎnshuò zhe jīngguāng.
examples.english: The night view of the city sparkles brightly.
